transcript: [speed-shifted, reversed] [excerpt audio from an adaptation of Douglas Adams’ The Restaurant at the End of the Universe, Chapter 17]
I think it’s pretty crazy that you ordered four peacocks and then—
I think you’re pretty crazy, too.
You better pay for what you ordered. That my point I’m tryin’ to make.
Well, I’ll tell ya. I didn’t order it. So I don’t know what the hell to tell ya.
I’m gonna send a couple security guards down there.
‘Kay, bring your paperwork with ya. Don’t forget the paperwork. Once that paperwork gets here, we’ll straighten that out real goddamn quick.
I’m gonna file a lawsuit against ya.
You can try it. You ain’t getting shit off o’ me!
[speed-shifted, reversed] [excerpt audio from an adaptation of Douglas Adams’ The Restaurant at the End of the Universe, Chapter 17]
I’ll tell you what, what’s your number over there? Lemme call you back.
And that’ll get me right to you?
“93-90”. 1-800-PICK-UPS and then when the operator comes up, punch in 93-90—
And that gets me to where?
It’s gonna get you to my kneebone in just a second.
Okay, do you get off on this shit? Is this what you do all day?
[speed-shifted, reversed] [excerpt audio from an adaptation of Douglas Adams’ The Restaurant at the End of the Universe, Chapter 17]
[sampled audio, source unknown]Quit yo jibber jabber!
[sampled audio, source unknown to transcriber]Quit yo—! Quit yo jibber jabber!
Ah! Let’s do this for, like, an hour, man. I love it!
[speed-shifted, reversed] [excerpt audio from an adaptation of Douglas Adams’ The Restaurant at the End of the Universe, Chapter 17]
Oh, I’m sorry. I must’ve got the wrong Kurt.
No, this is me. What’s goin’ on?
Huh? This don’t sound like Kirk, man.
Uh, I’m gettin’ ready to go to Denny’s.
I just got me some menudo!
Fuck, I am hung over last night—
Fuh! So, you gonna meet us at Orange Julius later?
Yeah. Okay. [titters] I think I got the wrong number, dude.
[speed-shifted] [excerpt audio from an adaptation of Douglas Adams’ The Restaurant at the End of the Universe, Chapter 17]
[vocalizing in quick gibberish approximating sped-up audio]
[speed-shifted, reversed] [excerpt audio from an adaptation of Douglas Adams’ The Restaurant at the End of the Universe, Chapter 17]

Hey, yeah. This me again. This here the guy that play the acoustic. Extended bass. Y’know mean? It’s like a good condition, the bass. Real clean and player, right? I think they got it wired up and everything. I see wires and stuff all up in there. This is where you can find all the black entertainers. They down here in east Texas.
[speed-shifted, reversed] [excerpt audio from an adaptation of Douglas Adams’ The Restaurant at the End of the Universe, Chapter 17]

Hi, my name is Ricky Knapp. I’m 16 years old, I live in Vancouver, Washington, and I heard you guys might be signing on new artists or you guys help people search for it. And so, here’s a little preview of what I can do. Here it goes:
[sings first verse and chorus of Sandy Mölling’s “One In A Million” a cappella]]

How did I get here?/ I turn around and there you are/ I didn’t think twice and rationalize/ ‘cause somehow I knew/ that there was more than just chemistry/ I mean, I knew you were kind of into me/ But I figured it was too good to be true/ I said pinch me. Where’s the catch this time?/ Can’t find a single cloud in the sky/ Help me before I get used to this guy/ And they say that good things take time/ But really great things happen in the blink of an eye/ Oh!
[speed-shifted, reversed] [excerpt audio from an adaptation of Douglas Adams’ The Restaurant at the End of the Universe, Chapter 17]

This is Johnathan Matthew. Sold 50,000 units of music on the streets of Amherst, Massachusetts. I am now spending most of my time alone and that’s all. I was lied about. And I don’t go out anymore ‘cause I don’t wanna be around people who are gonna believe a lie like that. Me, myself, I play, ah, keyboards, drum machines, turntables, oscillators, guitar, bass. I love to write drum beats on my little drum machine. I’m extremely prolific to the point of being, like, crazy. I’m crazy anyway and I like that. I don’t wanna be normal. Normal people scare me. I mean, my music really speaks loud. Please call me back.
[speed-shifted, reversed] [excerpt audio from an adaptation of Douglas Adams’ The Restaurant at the End of the Universe, Chapter 17]

[whispering] But anyway, you know, that’s over the North of West Sector. You know, at [unclear]. Just get over there. Get you a piece. Kick back, you know, and be cool and stuff. Be firin’ stuff up. Burn it up. Just burn it up everwhere. Big poop in that ass and stuff. A big poop in that ass. I’d even have ta get you a part if you wanted to. But fire it up. Get that fire goin’.
[speed-shifted, reversed] [excerpt audio from an adaptation of Douglas Adams’ The Restaurant at the End of the Universe, Chapter 17]

I don’t believe society exists. There’s no such thing as “society”. So— The idea of Society is something that was used by parents and teachers and clergymen and other authority figures in order to control children—as children are growing up, in order to believe that there’s something called Society. So that when they do grow up, they become, uh, complacent sheep and behave in these proscribed manners and—and build some sort of imaginary power structure that only benefits people in positions of power. But Power itself is an illusion. The only thing that Power means is that people agree somebody should be in power. And that’s how people like Jim Jones rise to Power. He gets a lot of people to agree that he should be in charge and that they should listen to him. And if you don’t like people like Jim Jones or Hitler or anybody in power, you don’t even fight with them. You just walk away from them and say, “I don’t need you. I don’t need your leadership. I don’t need you to tell me what to do.”
[speed-shifted] [excerpt audio from an adaptation of Douglas Adams’ The Restaurant at the End of the Universe, Chapter 17]
Okay, you’re making a collect call. This is a Sprint operator.
Yeah, is this the wrong number?
No, um, it’s been reported to me that there’s been several calls made to this number.
I’m trying to get through. I haven’t got through yet.
Okay, but the party that we’re calling keeps refusing the charges.
[speed-shifted, reversed] [excerpt audio from an adaptation of Douglas Adams’ The Restaurant at the End of the Universe, Chapter 17]
